steps.title: Count the tall trees
steps.content:
The condition is that the tree is $20$ feet or taller. Count only trees in that group:
$$\text{birch tall}=4\cdot9=36$$
$$\text{maple tall}=7\cdot6=42$$

steps.title: Form the conditional probability
steps.content:
Among trees at least $20$ feet tall, the total is:
$$36+42=78$$
The probability of maple is:
$$\frac{42}{78}=\frac{7}{13}$$
The answer is C.
